---
title: "Robinson-Foulds Distance Calculation: SimPhy Gene Trees vs True Species Tree"
format: html
jupyter: julia-1.10
---

## Robinson-Foulds Distance Calculation: SimPhy Gene Trees vs True Species Tree

```{julia}
using PhyloNetworks
using DataFrames
using CSV 
using Statistics 
using PhyloPlots
include("../../scripts/utilities.jl")
```


## Overview
- Under H = 0: All SNaQ trees found match the true species tree (RF = 0)
- Under H = 1: Some networks' displayed trees should differ from true tree
- Issue: Cases where both `RF_net1_1_true` AND `RF_net1_2_true` are non-zero are anomalous

When `RF_net1_1_true = 0` (true tree found in H1 major tree), we expect `RF_net1_2_true` should NOT be zero (minor tree differs). But if both are non-zero, both displayed trees differ from the true tree - this needs investigation.

```{julia}
df = CSV.read("/u/b/i/bingl/private/simulation-reptiles/output/DUP0.0-LOS0.0-RVGL-N_ind1-SF1.0/SNaQ-DUP0.0-LOS0.0-RVGL-N_ind1-SF1.0-summary.csv", DataFrame)

# H0 finds true tree 
H0_true = filter(row -> row.RF_net0_true == 0.0, df) 

# Both displayed trees differ from true tree
both_diff = filter(row -> (row.RF_net1_1_true != 0.0) && (row.RF_net1_2_true != 0.0), df)

# Only major tree matches true tree
major_matches = filter(row -> (row.RF_net1_1_true == 0.0) && (row.RF_net1_2_true != 0.0), df)

# Only minor tree matches true tree
minor_matches = filter(row -> (row.RF_net1_1_true != 0.0) && (row.RF_net1_2_true == 0.0), df)

println("H=0 finds true: $(nrow(H0_true))")
println("H=0 finds true; H=1 doesn't find the true: $(nrow(both_diff))")
println("Major matches: $(nrow(major_matches))")
println("Minor matches: $(nrow(minor_matches))")
```

```{julia}
# Examine specific anomalous cases
anomaly_cases = first(both_diff, 5).repID
```

```{julia}
# Function to load and display H1 network
function examine_h1_network(repID, paramname="DUP0.0-LOS0.0-RVGL-N_ind1-SF1.0")
    rep_H1_folder = "/u/b/i/bingl/private/simulation-reptiles/output/$paramname/rep$repID/snaqfolder/H1_output"
    rep_H0_folder = "/u/b/i/bingl/private/simulation-reptiles/output/$paramname/rep$repID/snaqfolder/H0_output"

    net_file = joinpath(rep_H1_folder, "H1.networks")
    tree_file = joinpath(rep_H0_folder, "H0.out")
    true_species_tree = readnewick("(A,((((B,C),(D,E)),F),(G,H)));") 

    if isfile(net_file)

        net = readnewick(net_file)
        tree_H0 = readnewick(tree_file)

        displayed_trees = displayedtrees(net, 0.0)
        rf0 = mudistance_semidirected(tree_H0, true_species_tree, preorder=true)
        rf1 = mudistance_semidirected(displayed_trees[1], true_species_tree, preorder=true)
        rf2 = mudistance_semidirected(displayed_trees[2], true_species_tree, preorder=true)
        
        println("H0 Tree (RF=$rf0)")
        println("H1 Major Tree (RF=$rf1)")
        println("H1 Minor Tree (RF=$rf2)")
        (tree_H0, displayed_trees, rf0, rf1, rf2)
    else
        return nothing
    end
end
```

```{julia}
function remove_branch_length_from_tree(net) 
    tips = tipLabels(net) 
    sorted_tips = sort(tips) 
    rootatnode!(net,"A") 
    newick = writeTopology(net) 
    replace(newick, r"[0-9\+\-\.\:]+" => "")
end
```

# Test three replicates where the displayed_trees under h = 0 does not recover the tree tree (rep = 008, 012, 013)

```{julia}
# Examine replicate 008 
result = examine_h1_network("008") 

if result !== nothing
    tree_H0, displayed_trees, rf0, rf1, rf2 = result
    println("RF distance for H = 0: $rf0") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 major: $rf1") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 minor: $rf2") 

    println("H=0 tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(tree_H0))
    println("H=1 major tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[1]))
    println("H=1 minor tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[2]))

end
```

```{julia}
result = examine_h1_network("011") 

if result !== nothing
    tree_H0, displayed_trees, rf0, rf1, rf2 = result
    println("RF distance for H = 0: $rf0") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 major: $rf1") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 minor: $rf2") 

    println("H=0 tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(tree_H0))
    println("H=1 major tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[1]))
    println("H=1 minor tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[2]))
end 

``` 

```{julia} 
result = examine_h1_network("012") # This replicate shows

if result !== nothing
    tree_H0, displayed_trees, rf0, rf1, rf2 = result
    println("RF distance for H = 0: $rf0") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 major: $rf1") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 minor: $rf2") 

    println("H=0 tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(tree_H0))
    println("H=1 major tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[1])) # This matches the real tree 
    println("H=1 minor tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[2])) # This has a large discrepency 
end 
```

```{julia} 
result = examine_h1_network("023") # This replicate shows

if result !== nothing
    tree_H0, displayed_trees, rf0, rf1, rf2 = result
    println("RF distance for H = 0: $rf0") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 major: $rf1") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 minor: $rf2") 

    println("H=0 tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(tree_H0))
    println("H=1 major tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[1])) # This matches the real tree 
    println("H=1 minor tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[2])) # This has a large discrepency 
end 
```

It seems that it is always F got misplaced under H = 1. 

# Test a replicate where the major tree shows the true tree 

```{julia}
result = examine_h1_network("013") # This replicate shows

if result !== nothing
    tree_H0, displayed_trees, rf0, rf1, rf2 = result
    println("RF distance for H = 0: $rf0") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 major: $rf1") 
    println("RF distance for H = 1 minor: $rf2") 

    println("H=0 tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(tree_H0))
    println("H=1 major tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[1])) # This matches the real tree 
    println("H=1 minor tree: ", remove_branch_length_from_tree(displayed_trees[2])) # This has a large discrepency 
end 
```
